qa_pair.question: A user asks whether CPT 76942 is covered in Texas, whether prior authorization is required, and wants related policy evidence. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.

A user wants to search Medicare LCDs, fetch policy L33831, search criteria text, or map states to MAC jurisdictions. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.

A user wants claim coverage status, prior-auth requirement, documentation requirements, denial risk, and optional policy-criteria evaluation for procedure and diagnosis codes. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.
A user wants to start payer website research for prior authorization or poll a prior-auth research_id. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.

A user wants commercial pharmacy benefit evidence from CVS Caremark, Express Scripts, or UnitedHealthcare / Optum Rx. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.
A user wants compliance stats, unreviewed policy changes, or to acknowledge policy changes. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.
A user wants to list, create, update, delete, or test webhook endpoints. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.
A user wants to diagnose whether the Verity API and dependencies are healthy. Which workflow-level Verity MCP tool should be used? Answer with the exact tool name only.
A user wants machine-readable output instead of readable Markdown from a Verity MCP tool. Which optional argument should they pass? Answer with the exact argument name only.
Is this server's shipped tool surface a workflow-level surface rather than a 1:1 endpoint-wrapper surface? Answer yes or no only.
